chunk_summary: This section highlights national program linkages in India for adolescent nutrition, focusing on WIFS adherence, deworming, and school meal access to improve follow-up success and prevent ineffective interventions.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of Weekly Iron and Folic Acid Supplementation (WIFS) in adolescent nutrition care.
Explore the PM-POSHAN scheme's contribution to school meal programs for adolescents.
Analyze the importance of deworming in preventing nutritional deficiencies among adolescents.
Evaluate the integration of national guidelines for controlling iron deficiency anemia in adolescent health services.
key_facts: The Weekly Iron and Folic Acid Supplementation (WIFS) program targets anemia prevention in adolescents.
Deworming is a critical intervention to reduce nutritional deficiencies caused by parasitic infections.
The PM-POSHAN scheme provides school meals to improve adolescent nutrition and educational outcomes.
RKSK guidelines emphasize adolescent-friendly health services for better program delivery.
Iron deficiency anemia control guidelines are issued by the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (MoHFW).
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Documenting program linkage (WIFS adherence, deworming, and school meal access) improves follow-up success and prevents repeated 'treatment without delivery.'
document_key_topics: Weekly Iron and Folic Acid Supplementation (WIFS)
